import { EmbeddingModelV3 } from '../../embedding-model/v3/embedding-model-v3';
import { ImageModelV3 } from '../../image-model/v3/image-model-v3';
import { LanguageModelV3 } from '../../language-model/v3/language-model-v3';
import { RerankingModelV3 } from '../../reranking-model/v3/reranking-model-v3';
import { SpeechModelV3 } from '../../speech-model/v3/speech-model-v3';
import { TranscriptionModelV3 } from '../../transcription-model/v3/transcription-model-v3';

/**
 * Provider for language, text embedding, and image generation models.
 */
export interface ProviderV3 {
  readonly specificationVersion: 'v3';

  /**
   * Returns the language model with the given id.
   * The model id is then passed to the provider function to get the model.
   *
   * @param {string} modelId - The id of the model to return.
   *
   * @returns {LanguageModel} The language model associated with the id
   *
   * @throws {NoSuchModelError} If no such model exists.
   */
  languageModel(modelId: string): LanguageModelV3;

  /**
   * Returns the text embedding model with the given id.
   * The model id is then passed to the provider function to get the model.
   *
   * @param {string} modelId - The id of the model to return.
   *
   * @returns {LanguageModel} The language model associated with the id
   *
   * @throws {NoSuchModelError} If no such model exists.
   */
  embeddingModel(modelId: string): EmbeddingModelV3;

  /**
   * Returns the text embedding model with the given id.
   * The model id is then passed to the provider function to get the model.
   *
   * @param {string} modelId - The id of the model to return.
   *
   * @returns {EmbeddingModel} The embedding model associated with the id
   *
   * @throws {NoSuchModelError} If no such model exists.
   *
   * @deprecated Use `embeddingModel` instead.
   */
  textEmbeddingModel?(modelId: string): EmbeddingModelV3;

  /**
   * Returns the image model with the given id.
   * The model id is then passed to the provider function to get the model.
   *
   * @param {string} modelId - The id of the model to return.
   *
   * @returns {ImageModel} The image model associated with the id
   */
  imageModel(modelId: string): ImageModelV3;

  /**
   * Returns the transcription model with the given id.
   * The model id is then passed to the provider function to get the model.
   *
   * @param {string} modelId - The id of the model to return.
   *
   * @returns {TranscriptionModel} The transcription model associated with the id
   */
  transcriptionModel?(modelId: string): TranscriptionModelV3;

  /**
   * Returns the speech model with the given id.
   * The model id is then passed to the provider function to get the model.
   *
   * @param {string} modelId - The id of the model to return.
   *
   * @returns {SpeechModel} The speech model associated with the id
   */
  speechModel?(modelId: string): SpeechModelV3;

  /**
   * Returns the reranking model with the given id.
   * The model id is then passed to the provider function to get the model.
   *
   * @param {string} modelId - The id of the model to return.
   *
   * @returns {RerankingModel} The reranking model associated with the id
   *
   * @throws {NoSuchModelError} If no such model exists.
   */
  rerankingModel?(modelId: string): RerankingModelV3;
}

I see - `ProviderV3` has `specificationVersion: 'v3'` as a property and `imageModel(modelId: string): ImageModelV3` method. My `PixelArtProvider` interface extends `ProviderV3`, so I need to make sure it has `specificationVersion`.

Wait, looking at the error again:
```
src/pixelart-provider.ts(77,5): error TS2353: Object literal may only specify known properties, and 'specificationVersion' does not exist in type 'PixelArtProvider'.


```

This says `specificationVersion` does NOT exist in `PixelArtProvider`. But `PixelArtProvider extends ProviderV3` which does have `specificationVersion`. The error is saying that when I return the object literal from `createPixelArt`, TypeScript doesn't recognize `specificationVersion` as a valid property on `PixelArtProvider` even though it should be inherited. This might be a TypeScript version issue or something about how the interface inheritance is being resolved.

Actually, I think the real problem is module resolution - if `ProviderV3` can't be found, then `PixelArtProvider` doesn't actually extend anything useful. The root issue is that I ran tsc globally without building the referenced packages first. The `tsconfig.json` uses project references to `../provider` and `../provider-utils`, which need to be compiled before TypeScript can type-check pixelart properly. Let me build those packages first so their type definitions are available.

```

The root cause is that when the `ProviderV3` import fails, my `PixelArtProvider` interface loses the `specificationVersion` property, making it an excess property error when I try to include it in the return statement. ## Summary: `packages/pixelart/` — 9 new files created

### Package Infrastructure
| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `package.json` | `@ai-sdk/pixelart` package — depends on `@ai-sdk/provider` and `@ai-sdk/provider-utils` workspace packages |
| `tsconfig.json` | Composite TypeScript project with references to `../provider` and `../provider-utils` |
| `tsconfig.build.json` | Disables composite mode for tsup builds |
| `tsup.config.ts` | Builds CJS + ESM + `.d.ts`, injects `__PACKAGE_VERSION__` |

### Source Files
| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `src/version.ts` | Version injection shim — reads `__PACKAGE_VERSION__` or falls back to `'0.0.0-test'` |
| `src/pixelart-image-settings.ts` | `PixelArtImageModelId` type alias and `PixelArtImageSettings` interface |
| `src/pixelart-image-model.ts` | `PixelArtImageModel` implementing `ImageModelV3` — POSTs to `/generate`, returns base64 images |
| `src/pixelart-provider.ts` | `PixelArtProvider` interface, `createPixelArt()` factory, default `pixelart` instance |
| `src/index.ts` | Barrel re-exports |

### Key Design Points

- **`PixelArtImageModel`** implements `ImageModelV3` (`specificationVersion: 'v3'`), POSTs JSON to `https://api.pixelart.ai/v1/generate`
- **Provider options** (`size`, `style: "pixel"|"retro"|"8bit"`, `pixelDensity`) validated via `lazySchema`/`zodSchema`, passed as `providerOptions.pixelart`
- Response is parsed as `{ images: [{ b64_json: string }] }` and returned as `Array<string>` (base64)
- Emits `SharedV3Warning` for unsupported features: `aspectRatio`, `files`, and `mask`
- `createPixelArt()` reads `PIXELART_API_KEY` from env for auth (`Authorization: Bearer <key>`)
- `languageModel()` and `embeddingModel()` throw `NoSuchModelError` — image-only provider
- **No existing files were modified**
